source_policy: Prefer official public Steam endpoints and pages for game metadata.
Do not copy raw long descriptions, review text, proprietary assets, binaries, source code, or decompiled content.
Treat prices, review counts, recommendations, player counts, and achievement percentages as volatile as-of metadata.
Treat achievement names as public names; classify gameplay effects only when another source or safe local metadata confirms them.
Fetch non-Steam public pages during refresh and fail if marker text for cited claims disappears.

notes: The full game has public achievement display rows and no-key global percentage data.
The demo global percentage endpoint is recorded with its current HTTP status and currently contributes no achievement ids.
Community-page percentages and API percentages can differ slightly due to cache timing or rounding.

public_gameplay_claims.claim: The player controls Froggy in a snowy desert survival loop.
public_gameplay_claims.notes: Public store copy supports the protagonist and setting; exact character roster remains unverified.

public_gameplay_claims.claim: Runs involve digging through snow, collecting resources, returning value home, and growing stronger.
public_gameplay_claims.notes: Use as high-level loop wording only; exact stats and route optimization need gameplay verification.

public_gameplay_claims.claim: Official Steam store copy supports home-base/resource-deposit structure, snow-as-cover combat use, Peaceful Mode puzzle pacing, and broad enemy/boss behavior variety.
public_gameplay_claims.notes: Use as broad structure and behavior-scope evidence only; exact layouts, puzzle solutions, weaknesses, and attack timing need gameplay verification.
Exact meter thresholds and upgrade effects remain unverified.

public_gameplay_claims.claim: Keys, treasure chests, artifacts, gems, traps, anomaly zones, bosses, and an escape door are public concepts.
public_gameplay_claims.notes: Use as entity scaffolding; do not invent quantities, costs, or drop rates.

public_gameplay_claims.claim: Peaceful Mode is public and described as monster-free.
public_gameplay_claims.notes: Mode-specific rewards, unlocks, and achievement eligibility still need verification.

public_gameplay_claims.claim: The full game exposes 42 public Steam achievements.
public_gameplay_claims.notes: Achievement percentages are volatile and should be refreshed before percentage-based claims.

public_gameplay_claims.claim: Steam news confirms 10 playable frogs, 16 locations, 60+ skills/tools/attacks/companions, and demo progress carryover.
public_gameplay_claims.notes: Use these as scope counts only; the complete named roster still needs verification.

public_gameplay_claims.claim: Steam devlogs say the ten playable frogs have unique specializations, main attacks, and starting skillsets, with public examples including tongue attacks, spits, snow minigun, electric staff, and hockey stick.
public_gameplay_claims.notes: Examples are public, but exact character-to-attack mapping still needs gameplay or safe local metadata verification.

public_gameplay_claims.claim: Steam Devlog #4 previews new enemies and bosses, new attack patterns/projectile types, new frog movement and snow-digging skills, and new companions including animals and robots.
public_gameplay_claims.notes: Use as broad content-category evidence only; the post does not name specific enemies, bosses, patterns, or projectile types.

public_gameplay_claims.claim: Steam demo/devlog posts describe Puff, quest-based meta-progression, and Blue Gems as unlock resources for characters, abilities, and locations.
public_gameplay_claims.notes: Exact quest names, Blue Gem costs, and unlock order still need verification.

public_gameplay_claims.claim: An Xbox Wire developer interview corroborates 16 maps, ten frogs, more than 60 tools/skills/companions, and companion roles including digging, gem collection, and environment scanning.
public_gameplay_claims.notes: Use as an official interview cross-check behind primary Steam sources.

public_gameplay_claims.claim: Steam devlogs confirm specific launch additions including Glider, Snowball Roll, Leap Chain, Delivery Bot, Flamethrower Bot, Scanner Drone, Piercing Icicles, Fireworks, and Snowball Volley.
public_gameplay_claims.notes: Names and broad categories are public; exact stats, rarity, and unlock requirements need gameplay verification.

public_gameplay_claims.claim: Steam devlogs confirm anomalous zones as short challenge areas with rewards including Blue Gems and artifacts in Common, Uncommon, Rare, and Legendary tiers.
public_gameplay_claims.notes: Challenge examples are public; exact layouts, timers, and reward quantities need verification.

public_gameplay_claims.claim: Steam snow-system devlog confirms snow as an interactable heightmap-like material with density/layers and named digging or ice tools.
public_gameplay_claims.notes: Use for mechanics scaffolding; exact tool stats and upgrade scaling need verification.

public_gameplay_claims.claim: The first post-launch Steam update confirms Zippy, Invincible Roll, Energy Wave, Destructive Field, Armor, and early location progression changes.
public_gameplay_claims.notes: This is a post-launch state source and should be refreshed when later patch notes are added.

notes: Steam news/devlog text is treated as public first-party source material, but exact numeric balance values still need gameplay or patch-note verification.
Counts from news/devlogs establish scope; they do not provide complete names for every frog, location, skill, tool, attack, or companion.
All Steam News API items are recorded with a classification so weak marketing/event posts are explicit instead of silently ignored.

research_gaps: Exact character/frog roster beyond Froggy, Puff, Zippy, and the verified 10-playable-frog count, including which frog uses each public main-attack example.
Named map/location roster, map unlock order beyond public thresholds/update notes, and location-specific boss names.
Named enemy roster, named boss roster, and exact attack-pattern/projectile behavior beyond Devlog #4's broad preview.
Exact stats, costs, cooldowns, drop rates, and upgrade tree order.
Whether demo metadata differs from the released full game after SteamCMD/local file extraction works.
Gameplay screenshots or short notes that can confirm inferred achievement-derived categories.
